title: Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner for home use. Runtim
stakeholder_needs: Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner for home use. Runtime >= 20 min, weight < 1.5 kg, EU safety standards.
analysis:
Stakeholders and needs:
- End users (home consumers): Want effective cleaning (suction/airflow, attachments for floors/sofa/car), >=20 min real-world runtime, low weight (<1.5 kg) for one-handed use, low noise, easy emptying, washable filters, clear battery status, fast convenient charging via plug-in base, good ergonomics (balance/grip), and reliability.
- Safety/compliance stakeholders (regulators, notified bodies, retailers): Require conformity to EU safety and compliance: CE marking; Electrical safety (LVD 2014/35/EU if applicable to charger/base; often external PSU under LVD), EMC (2014/30/EU), RoHS (2011/65/EU), WEEE (2012/19/EU), REACH substances, Battery Regulation (EU) 2023/1542, and UN 38.3 for lithium transport. Harmonized standards typically include EN 60335-1 and EN 60335-2-2 (vacuum cleaners), EN 62233 (EMF), and relevant EN 55014 / EN 61000 series for EMC; plus IEC 62133-2 for Li-ion cell safety (commonly used).
- Manufacturer/engineering: Need clear performance targets and test conditions (runtime definition, suction power, dust pickup), manufacturability, serviceability, cost/BOM targets, supply chain for battery cells/chargers, and quality controls.
- Retailers/distributors: Need robust packaging, safe shipping of Li-ion, clear labeling/manuals in EU languages, warranty/returns handling.
- Environment/sustainability stakeholders: Repairability (battery/filter replacement), recyclability, compliance with WEEE and battery collection, energy efficiency of charger (ErP/ecodesign for external power supplies if applicable).
Key requirement implications from provided constraints:
- Runtime >=20 min must be tied to a specific mode (standard vs boost) and load condition; battery capacity and motor efficiency must meet it.
- Weight <1.5 kg likely constrains battery size, motor, and plastics; may imply stick vs handheld-only configuration and accessory set.
- Indoor 10–40°C requires battery and plastics rated for that ambient, plus charging safety across that range.
- Plug-in charging base introduces requirements for charger safety, docking stability, and user interaction; may use external certified PSU to simplify compliance.
clarification_questions: How is the 20+ minute runtime defined: in which power mode (standard/eco vs boost), with which attachment, and with what test method/conditions (e.g., EN 60335-2-2 based, empty bin, new filter, ambient temperature)?

What cleaning performance targets are required beyond runtime/weight (e.g., minimum suction power in kPa or air watts, airflow, dust pickup on hard floor vs carpet, debris volume/bin capacity, and acceptable noise level)?

What battery/charging expectations should be met (Li-ion chemistry preference, target charge time, replaceable vs sealed battery, charging in 10–40°C, and whether the charger should be an external off-the-shelf CE-certified PSU or integrated)?
